assertions.title: Credit Costs Shown in Model Dropdowns

steps.observed: Dashboard displayed with Create Project button

steps.expected: Project setup wizard opens on Concept tab
steps.observed: Wizard opened on Concept tab (Step 1 of 4)
Three dropdowns visible: Storyline, Image, Video models

steps.action: Verify dropdown labels show credit costs
steps.observed: All dropdowns show correct format: 'Llama 3.3 70B Versatile (1 credits)', 'Nano Banana 2 (4 credits)', 'Kling O3 Standard T2V (20 credits)'
All values match: Nano Banana 2=4, Kling O3 Standard T2V=20, FLUX Schnell=3, etc. All 10 image generation models and 17 video generation models listed with correct credits.

steps.expected: Default form with Upload Document button
steps.observed: Upload Document button visible at bottom of concept textarea
documentService.ts defines ACCEPTED_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS = '.pdf,.docx,.md,.txt,.text'
CLIENT_PARSEABLE_EXTENSIONS = ['txt', 'md', 'text', 'markdown'] — reads via readTextFileLocally()
Non-client-parseable files sent to supabase.functions.invoke('document-parse') with base64 body
Text content loaded into textarea: 'test concept content for validation testing' (43 chars)

assertions.title: Document Upload Shows Loading and Error States

steps.observed: isParsingDocument state drives Loader2 spinner with 'Parsing...' text in DynamicConceptForm.tsx (DefaultConceptForm)

steps.expected: Toast error on failure, textarea unchanged
steps.observed: try/catch block calls toast.error(msg) on failure; textarea only updated on success (updateProjectData only called in try block)
documentService.validateFile() throws 'File is too large' for files > MAX_FILE_SIZE (10MB)
validateFile() throws 'Unsupported file type' for non-supported extensions

evidence.codeInspection: src/services/documentService.ts: validateFile() checks size and extension; DynamicConceptForm.tsx: isParsingDocument state shows Loader2 + 'Parsing...', catch block calls toast.error()

assertions.issues: Loading state could not be visually captured since TXT files parse instantly client-side and PDF/DOCX require the edge function. Code inspection confirms the loading indicator and error handling are properly implemented.

assertions.title: Commercial Format Has Product Image Upload

steps.observed: Ad Brief Builder form displayed with orange gradient header

steps.action: Verify Product Reference Image upload field
steps.observed: 'PRODUCT REFERENCE IMAGE (OPTIONAL)' section with dashed border upload area: 'Upload product image' / 'or click to browse'

steps.action: Verify image URL storage in code
steps.observed: handleProductImageUpload() creates objectURL and stores in adBrief.productImageUrl via updateProjectData()
ProjectContext.tsx: structuredPayload.concept.adBrief includes productImageUrl when format is 'commercial'

assertions.title: Music Video Format Has BPM Detection

steps.observed: Music Video Brief form displayed with pink gradient header

steps.expected: Audio track upload area with BPM detection label
steps.observed: 'AUDIO TRACK (FOR BPM DETECTION)' section with upload area: 'Upload audio for BPM detection' / 'MP3, WAV, or other audio formats'
MusicVideoForm uses 'import { analyze } from web-audio-beat-detector', detects tempo, rounds to integer BPM
After detection: '{musicData.bpm} BPM detected' text displayed, beat timeline visualization with beat markers
updateMusicDataBatch stores bpm, beatTimeline in musicVideoData; structuredPayload.concept.musicVideoData includes it

evidence.codeInspection: src/components/project-setup/DynamicConceptForm.tsx: MusicVideoForm imports web-audio-beat-detector, displays BPM and beat timeline

assertions.issues: Audio file upload testing blocked — no real audio file available in test environment. UI field presence and code logic verified.

assertions.title: Infotainment Format Has Required Capture Fields

steps.observed: Infotainment Brief form displayed with amber/orange gradient header
'TOPIC *' input field present with placeholder 'e.g., The Science of Sleep'

steps.expected: Target audience input with required marker
steps.observed: 'TARGET AUDIENCE *' input field present with placeholder 'e.g., Curious adults 25-45'

steps.expected: Key facts textarea with required marker
steps.observed: 'KEY FACTS *' textarea present with placeholder about essential facts and statistics
'VISUAL STYLE' dropdown with options: Animated Explainer, Documentary Footage, Whiteboard, Mixed Media, Infographic, Cinematic
updateInfoData() stores all fields in infotainmentData object via updateProjectData()

assertions.title: All Concept Prompts Use Structured JSON

steps.observed: structuredPayload object with nested concept, storyline, settings, cast sections — each with explicit field mapping
Body: { project_id, generate_alternative? } — structured JSON, not string concatenation
Body: { fileName, mimeType, fileData } — structured JSON
Called without body — no prompt concatenation
CharacterCard.tsx: supabase.functions.invoke with body object

steps.action: Search for string concatenation in prompts
steps.observed: No evidence of string concatenation for generation prompts. All payloads use object-based JSON bodies.

evidence.codeInspection: src/components/project-setup/ProjectContext.tsx lines 370-405: structuredPayload with concept, storyline, settings, cast sections. All supabase.functions.invoke calls use structured body objects.

assertions.title: Credit Costs Sourced from Single Constants File

steps.action: Verify studio-model-constants.ts as source of truth
steps.observed: src/lib/studio-model-constants.ts contains complete model catalog with credits for all image, video, audio, text models
src/lib/constants/credits.ts re-exports from studio-model-constants.ts, provides formatModelLabel(), getModelCredits(), buildCreditsMap()
ConceptTab imports formatModelLabel from credits.ts, getModelsByTypeAndGroup from studio-model-constants.ts — all correct
ImagesToVideoNode.tsx:158 has hardcoded '2 credits' and line 49 has hardcoded creditUsed value of 2

steps.expected: Billing pack prices are different from model costs
steps.observed: FALLBACK_PACKS contains billing product data (pack_50: 50 credits for $5) — these are billing products, not model generation costs, so acceptable

evidence.codeInspection: src/components/nodes/ImagesToVideoNode.tsx:48-49 hardcodes 'video' cost as 2 credits; line 158 displays '2 credits' as hardcoded string. This should source from studio-model-constants.ts.

assertions.issues: ImagesToVideoNode.tsx has hardcoded credit value '2 credits' at line 158 and passes literal 2 as cost at line 49. This should reference the studio-model-constants.ts catalog or credits.ts helpers instead.

frictions.description: Upload Document button creates a dynamic file input via JavaScript rather than having a persistent file input in the DOM, making it impossible to use agent-browser upload command directly. Had to verify upload via code inspection and simulated content loading.
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript eval to simulate text content loading and verified upload mechanism via source code inspection

frictions.description: BPM detection requires a real audio file which is not available in the test environment
frictions.resolution: Verified audio upload field presence in UI and BPM detection code logic via source inspection

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 7 passed, 1 failed. VAL-SETUP-001 through VAL-SETUP-007 all pass — model dropdowns show correct credit costs from constants, document upload works with proper accept types and loading/error states, Commercial has product image upload, Music Video has BPM detection UI, Infotainment has all required fields, and all payloads use structured JSON. VAL-CROSS-015 fails due to hardcoded '2 credits' in ImagesToVideoNode.tsx (should source from constants).
